vcgs.description: Prosperity-Induced Heart-Elevation — pride as the heart lifted up by material satisfaction, success, or divine blessing; the satisfaction-to-self-exaltation dynamic where prosperity corrupts the inward orientation toward God.

Royal and Conquering Heart-Elevation — the elevation of a ruler's, conqueror's, or prince's heart above proper creaturely and divine limits; pride of those who hold power or achieve victory and translate their elevated position into a claim of supremacy or divine status.

The Proud Heart as Constitutive Inner Condition — pride as a settled, dispositional characteristic of the heart not tied to a specific trigger or ruling subject, but named as the constitutional inner condition defining a person's orientation before God; heart-pride as abomination, precursor to ruin, and source of contemptuous speech.

M08-A2.characteristic: CHAR-1 (Arrogant self-elevation — seat: eyes and outward bearing)

vcgs.description: Haughty Eyes — Pride in the Gaze — verses specifically naming the eyes or gaze as the register of pride; haughty eyes as what God abhors, actively brings down, and contrasts with humility's posture.

Outward Display and Body-Language of Pride — pride's visible register in broader bearing, ornament, and posture: the necklace-like display, ostentatious posture and wanton glances, haughty face-posture shutting out God, and head lifted in self-exaltation.

Moab's Pride and Neighbouring Nations — publicly-known collective arrogance as a reported, multi-layered inner characteristic underlying haughtiness and empty boasting (Moab, Edom, Ammon); the paradigm OT case of national pride.

Babylon's Pride — National Splendor Destined for Total Ruin — corporate arrogance as self-glorying magnificence that makes the coming overthrow exemplary; the proud crown of Ephraim as fading flower; Sodom's haughtiness expressing in abomination.

Israel's Pride — The Self-Incriminating Inner Characteristic of God's Own People — pride that testifies against Israel to its own face, blocks repentance, drives idolatry, and provokes divine revulsion; also Philistia and Assyria as further national subjects.

Day-of-the-LORD Universal Collective Humbling — everything proud and lofty targeted by divine judgment; the cosmic register where all collective pride — not one nation but all human self-elevation — is brought low on the eschatological day.

M08-A4.characteristic: CHAR-1 (Arrogant self-elevation — seat: general dispositional individual)

vcgs.description: Self-Love as Root and NT Vice-Catalogue Pride — filautos as the generative root; NT vice-catalogue huperēfanos and hupsēlos naming arrogance among the defining vices of moral disorder, last-days character, and worldly self-orientation; Mar 7:22 as the heart-sourced vice statement.

God Actively Opposing the Proud — Divine Opposition as the Structural Frame — verses where God's watchful eyes on the haughty, God's challenge to abase the proud, and God's keeping of the haughty at relational distance constitute the primary framing content.

Psalmic Enemy Portraits — Pride as Predatory Social Aggression — arrogance as the inner driving force of oppressors: pursuing the poor, trampling, deceiving, speaking from felt superiority, crushing the psalmist's soul; the relational destructiveness of the arrogant disposition.

Wisdom Maxims — Pride's Self-Defeating Consequences — Proverbs, Job, Ecclesiastes, and Daniel evidencing pride as a structurally self-defeating inner disposition: precedes destruction, brings bearer low, blocks divine response, blinds to truth, is a habitual posture God can humble.

Individual Self-Exaltation Against God — The Willed Act of Inner Elevation — the high-hand posture against God, the Psa 75 complex prohibiting self-elevation as God's exclusive prerogative, the Assyrian's arrogant mockery against the Holy One, and the paradigm volitional self-exaltation (Isa 14:13 — will declaring intent to self-exalt to heaven).

Boiling Presumption — the Zid Register — pride as a will that 'boils over' against a known divine prohibition or command; the deliberate, willful quality of transgression against God's explicit word and appointed order.

Arrogant Insolence Against Authority — the Zed/Za.don Register — arrogant presumption against God's law, appointed religious and legal authority, and divine revelation; pride as contempt-for-authority producing predatory action against the law-keeper and the faithful.

Self-Willed Presumption in NT Narrative — domineering authority, self-willed dismissal of legitimate constraint, and irreverent boldness: archō M08-relational verses (domineering, presumptuous assertion against Jesus' path, rivalry over preeminence), authadēs (self-will dismissing all authority), tolmētēs (reckless presumption against divine beings).

M08-C.cross_register_flag: M22 (praise/thanksgiving/glory) — God-directed glorying register in VCG-03

vcgs.description: Condemned Self-Directed Boasting — ha.lal self-boasting (premature glorification, boasting in creaturely assets, empty self-promotion), alazoneia/alazōn inner boasting disposition, and kauchaomai in its condemned form (Law-grounded pride, grace excluding works-boasting, the rivals' self-serving assertions); ro.hav as the ironic undercut.

The Pauline Boasting Discourse — Paul's extended examination of legitimate vs illegitimate self-commendation in 2 Corinthians 10–12: bounded boasting within God-given spheres, exposure of rivals' self-serving assertions, the fool's-speech inversion, weakness as the content of boasting, the cross displacing self as the object of pride. M22 cross-register present in the God-directed concluding principle (2Cor 10:17).

Grace-Grounded and God-Directed Boasting — the M22 cross-register — boasting directed wholly toward God (Rom 5:2–3, 5:11; 1Cor 1:31; Phili 3:3), faith's logic eliminating human self-glorification (Rom 3:27), relational and eschatological boasting in others' faithfulness (2Cor 7:4; 7:14; 1Th 2:19; Phili 1:26; 2:16), hope as the persevering inner grip on the promise (Heb 3:6). Cross-register flag: M22 explicitly marks this VCG.

Intellectual and Partisan Conceit — inflation through knowledge (1Cor 8:1 — knowledge inflates while love builds up), factional partisan pride (1Cor 4:6), contemptuous self-confidence (1Cor 4:18), empty boasting exposed (1Cor 4:19), arrogant inflation preventing proper grief (1Cor 5:2), and ungrounded mental fantasy (Col 2:18).

Conceit as Miscalibrated Self-Assessment — overreaching the measure of faith (Rom 12:3 huperfroneō), inflated self-regard leading to the devil's condemnation (1Ti 3:6 tufoō), conceit blinding to truth and fueling controversy (1Ti 6:4), swollen conceit distorting judgment (2Ti 3:4), and conceit as communal disorder (2Cor 12:20 fusiōsis).

National Proud Might — Strength and Power as Ground for Arrogant Self-Sufficiency — ga.on proud-might vocabulary: national military might, structural power, and collective strength becoming the inner ground for arrogance; Egypt's proud might, Israel's pride of power, Sodom's pride paired with material excess. M23 cross-register flag: pride-as-misuse-of-genuine-strength.

Wealth, Position, and Self-Secured Elevation — Material Pride — prosperity and physical fortification as the ground for inner arrogant self-sufficiency: wealth corrupting the heart (Eze 28:5), Babylon's voluptuous ease feeding prideful self-sufficiency (Isa 47:8), haughtiness tied to material prosperity (1Ti 6:17), fortified high ground as security delusion (Jer 49:16; Jer 51:53; Obd 3; Hab 2:9), imperious self-will acting without shame or restraint (Eze 16:30), and rulers' visible dominance (Eze 19:11). M23 cross-register flag.

Akratēs — Lack of Self-Control as Pride's Enabling Condition — the single BOUNDARY verse: lack of self-control appearing in the last-days vice list alongside huperēfanos and filautos; the will's inability to restrain impulses as the enabling condition through which pride operates unchecked. Researcher decision pending: M08 qualifying component or future self-control cluster.
